assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map 'Ambiguities Identified' section lists 5 ambiguities: (1) No performance target specified for 'faster' search, (2) No relevance criteria defined for 'more relevant' results, (3) Filter dimensions unspecified for 'some kind of filtering', (4) Search scope undefined across entity types, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality' lacks criteria. All 5 directly correspond to vague language in the feature description.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Excluded Requirements' section states: 'Better UI (non-MVP) | Cannot be planned: no frontend repository is available in the Repository Registry, and no design mockups or Figma URLs have been provided. UI improvements require a frontend repository and design specifications to decompose into actionable tasks. This requirement is explicitly excluded from scope.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All three task files contain the required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Verification Commands. Task 1 also includes Files to Create; Task 3 includes API Changes; Tasks 2 and 3 include Dependencies. Optional sections are omitted per template rules ('Omit sections that don't apply').

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 has two assumptions: 'Assumption (pending clarification): The primary performance bottleneck is at the database query level...' and 'Assumption (pending clarification): The search scope includes all entity types...'. Task 2 has two: relevance scoring using ts_rank and unified search scope. Task 3 has two: minimum viable filter set (entity type, date range) and AND semantics for filter combination. All are explicitly labeled 'Assumption (pending clarification)'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All three tasks begin with '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' — a single repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: has '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 44), '## Test Requirements' (line 51). Task 2: has all four sections at corresponding positions. Task 3: has all four sections at corresponding positions.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All modified files exist in repo-backend.md: modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (line 91), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (line 93), migration/src/lib.rs (line 17). Files to Create: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the established migration pattern from migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs (line 19-20). No invented paths were found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references: common/src/db/query.rs for shared query builder patterns, migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs for SeaORM migration pattern with 'Index::create()', tower-http caching middleware. Task 2 references: common/src/db/query.rs for sorting helpers, common/src/model/paginated.rs for PaginatedResults<T>, ts_rank(tsvector_column, to_tsquery(search_term)). Task 3 references: common/src/db/query.rs for filtering helpers, PaginatedResults<T> total count handling, common/src/error.rs::AppError for 400 responses.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence:
Task 1 line 4-5: '## Target Branch
main'. Task 2 line 4-5: '## Target Branch
main'. Task 3 line 4-5: '## Target Branch
main'.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: Three separate digest files exist: task-1-digest.md contains '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:c7fca41a63e23c8a6b80cd26c8e5fa0c9b147b38d04a9bb8285d400d95660ec1', task-2-digest.md contains 'sha256-md:795a49e21019e8112fe4d9269a866667d28ce281271aed29215d72b6a4989979', task-3-digest.md contains 'sha256-md:b7216609aeba6239e11828ba146660f4839b4e29c457faeca237b94881b2c227'. All three hashes are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:'.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention entries across all three tasks use the prescribed rationale format. Example from task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Error handling: ensure all new or modified handler functions return Result<T, AppError>... Applies: task modifies modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs matching the convention's Rust endpoint file scope.' No 'Not applicable' annotations appear anywhere. Each convention entry cites a specific file being modified and the convention's scope.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature priority is 'Normal' (not Undefined). impact-map.md Field Propagation section: 'Priority: Normal (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all created tasks)'. summary-comment.md: 'Priority: Normal (propagated from TC-9002 to all tasks). The Feature's priority is "Normal" which is a valid priority value (not "Undefined"), so it is included in additional_fields on each created task as "priority": {"name": "Normal"}'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Feature has fixVersions: 'RHTPA 1.6.0' (non-empty). No fixVersion scope config in CLAUDE.md Jira Configuration, so default 'both' applies. impact-map.md: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all created tasks; no fixVersion scope setting found in Jira Configuration, defaulting to "both")'. summary-comment.md: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (propagated from TC-9002 to all tasks)... the default scope of "both" applies — fixVersions are propagated to tasks as "fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.6.0"}]'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an 'Inherited Field Values' section with: 'Priority: Normal (propagated from TC-9002 to all tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (propagated from TC-9002 to all tasks)'. Additionally, impact-map.md contains a 'Field Propagation' section with both values.

assertion_results.text: No documentation task is generated because the feature description does not contain a Documentation Considerations section
assertion_results.evidence: Only three task files exist in outputs: task-1-optimize-search-performance.md (performance optimization), task-2-improve-search-relevance.md (relevance scoring), task-3-add-search-filters.md (search filters). None are documentation tasks. The summary-comment.md task table confirms only these three tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: No testing tasks are generated because no testing readiness template is present in the eval fixture files
assertion_results.evidence: Only three task files exist in outputs: task-1 (performance), task-2 (relevance), task-3 (filters). None are standalone testing tasks. While each task includes a 'Test Requirements' section (inline test criteria), no dedicated testing task was generated. The summary-comment.md confirms only 3 tasks were created, all implementation-focused.
